key_points: Coordinate geometry deals with the study of points on a plane using coordinates
The Cartesian plane is formed by two perpendicular number lines called X-axis and Y-axis
The point where X-axis and Y-axis intersect is called the origin
Coordinates of a point are written as an ordered pair (x, y)
The plane is divided into four regions called quadrants
formulas: Distance of a point (x, y) from X-axis = |y|
Distance of a point (x, y) from Y-axis = |x|
exam_tips: Always mention the ordered pair in correct order (x, y)
Check the signs of x and y to identify the correct quadrant
Use a proper scale while plotting points
Points on axes do not belong to any quadrant
